---
title: "Uncovering Patterns in MLB Performance Data"
author: "Nevena Ciganovic, Adele Lauzon, and Joanna Lo"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
```


## Load Data and Feature Engineering

```{r load-data-and-feature-engineering}
set.seed(2201)

# Load Statcast 2019 data
data <- read_csv("Statcast_2019.csv")

# Remove deprecated and irrelevant columns
data_cleaned <- data %>%
  select(-contains("deprecated"), -spin_dir, -umpire, -game_date, -game_type) %>%
  drop_na(release_speed)

# Feature engineering: create outcome labels and custom variables
data_cleaned <- data_cleaned %>%
  mutate(
    team_at_bat = ifelse(inning_topbot == "Top", "away", "home"),

      type_of_hit = case_when(
        events == "sac_fly_double_play" | events == "sac_fly" ~ "sac_fly",
        events == "sac_bunt_double_play" | events == "sac_bunt" ~ "sac_bunt",
        events == "home_run" ~ "home_run",
        events == "single" ~ "single",
        events == "double" ~ "double",
        events == "triple" ~ "triple"
      ),

      outcome = case_when(
        events == "home_run" ~ "home_run",
        events %in% c("single", "double", "triple") ~ "hit",
        events %in% c("field_out", "force_out", "fly_out", "grounded_into_double_play",
                     "lineout", "double_play", "strikeout", "strikeout_double_play",
                     "fielders_choice_out", "other_out") ~ "out"
      )
  )
```

## Data Description and Exploratory Plots

```{r data-description-and-exploratory-plots}
# Summary statistics of selected numeric features
data_cleaned %>%
  select(exit_velocity = launch_speed, launch_angle, release_speed,
         plate_x, plate_z, hit_distance_sc, woba_value) %>%
  summary()

# Plot 1: Exit velocity by batted ball outcome
ggplot(data_cleaned, aes(x = outcome, y = launch_speed)) +
   geom_boxplot(fill = "lightgreen") +
   labs(title = "Exit Velocity by Outcome", x = "Outcome", y = "Exit Velocity (mph)") +
   theme_minimal()

# Plot 2: Hit coordinates by type of hit
ggplot(filter(data_cleaned, !is.na(type_of_hit)),
       aes(x = hc_x, y = hc_y, color = type_of_hit)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.2, size = 0.8) +
  scale_y_reverse() +
  coord_fixed() +
  labs(title = "Hit Coordinates of Batted Balls", x = "X", y = "Y") +
  guides(colour = guide_legend(title = "Type of Hit", override.aes = list(alpha = 1))) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(panel.border = element_blank())

# Plot 3: wOBA by pitch type
ggplot(data_cleaned, aes(x = pitch_type, y = woba_value)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "lightblue") +
  labs(title = "wOBA by Pitch Type", x = "Pitch Type", y = "wOBA") +
  theme_minimal()
```

## Check Assumptions for Multinomial Logistic Regression

```{r check-assumptions-for-multinomial-logist}
# Load libraries
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)
library(corrplot)
library(ggplot2)
library(purrr)
library(nnet)

# 1. Check Multicollinearity

# Keep only numeric columns with more than one unique value and no all-NA columns
numeric_clean <- data_cleaned %>%
  dplyr::select(where(is.numeric)) %>%
  dplyr::select(where(~ !all(is.na(.)))) %>%
  dplyr::select(where(~ length(unique(.)) > 1)) 

# Compute correlation matrix
cor_matrix <- cor(numeric_clean, use = "complete.obs")

# Plot correlation matrix
corrplot::corrplot(
  cor_matrix,
  method = "color",
  tl.cex = 0.7,
  title = "Correlation Matrix of Numeric Features",
  mar = c(0, 0, 2, 0),
  na.label = " "  # optional: remove NA squares completely
)

# 2. Linearity of Log-Odds

# Extract numeric predictor names
numeric_vars <- data_cleaned %>%
  dplyr::select(where(is.numeric)) %>%
  names()

# Split features into chunks for better plotting
feature_chunks <- split(numeric_vars, ceiling(seq_along(numeric_vars) / 6))

# Density plots to visually check log-odds linearity
walk2(
  feature_chunks,
  seq_along(feature_chunks),
  ~{
    data_long <- data_cleaned %>%
      dplyr::select(all_of(.x), outcome) %>%
      pivot_longer(cols = -outcome, names_to = "feature", values_to = "value")
    
    p <- ggplot(data_long, aes(x = value, color = outcome)) +
      geom_density(na.rm = TRUE) +
      facet_wrap(~feature, scales = "free", ncol = 3) +
      theme_minimal() +
      labs(
        title = paste("Log-Odds Linearity Check: Group", .y),
        x = "Feature Value",
        y = "Density"
      )
    
    print(p)
  }
)
```

## Check Assumptions for LDA/QDA

```{r check-assumptions-for-lda-qda}
# Load libraries
library(MASS)
library(car)
library(ggpubr)
library(biotools)              # For Box s M test
library(caret)
library(tidyverse)

# Subset numeric features only
numeric_vars <- data_cleaned %>%
  dplyr::select(where(is.numeric)) %>%
  dplyr::select(-contains("deprecated"))

# Combine with outcome
lda_data <- bind_cols(numeric_vars, outcome = data_cleaned$outcome)

# Multivariate Normality Check
library(purrr)

# Split the data by class
split_data <- lda_data %>%
  group_by(outcome) %>%
  group_split()

# Apply Shapiro-Wilk test for each numeric column in each group
normality_results <- map_df(split_data, function(df) {
  outcome_label <- unique(df$outcome)
  numeric_cols <- df %>% dplyr::select(where(is.numeric))

     result <- map_dbl(numeric_cols, function(col) {
        if (length(na.omit(col)) >= 3 && length(na.omit(col)) <= 5000) {
          shapiro.test(col)$p.value
        } else {
        }
     })

     tibble(outcome = outcome_label, !!!as.list(result))
})

print(normality_results)


# Plot one feature to visualize normality
ggplot(lda_data, aes(x = launch_speed, fill = outcome)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "Density Plot of Launch Speed by Outcome")
```

## Models: Random Forest, XGBoost, Feedforward Neural Network

```{r models-random-forest-xgboost-feedforward}
# Load libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(caret)
library(recipes)
library(rsample)
library(randomForest)
library(xgboost)
library(nnet)
library(yardstick)
library(pROC)
library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)
library(doParallel)

# Register parallel backend
cl <- makePSOCKcluster(parallel::detectCores() - 1)

# Feature selection
selected_features <- c(
  "outcome",
  "launch_speed",
  "launch_angle",
  "hit_distance_sc",
  "bb_type",
  "plate_x",
  "plate_z",
  "hc_x",
  "hc_y",
  "stand",
  "pitch_type",
)

# Filter
swing_data <- data_cleaned %>%
  dplyr::select(all_of(selected_features)) %>%
  filter(complete.cases(.))

# Train-test split
set.seed(123)
split <- initial_split(swing_data, prop = 0.8, strata = outcome)
train_data <- training(split)
test_data <- testing(split)

# Preprocessing
rec <- recipe(outcome ~ ., data = train_data) %>%
  step_zv(all_predictors()) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal(), -all_outcomes()) %>%
  step_normalize(all_numeric(), -all_outcomes())

prep_rec <- prep(rec)
train_baked <- bake(prep_rec, new_data = NULL)
test_baked <- bake(prep_rec, new_data = test_data)

# CV
ctrl <- trainControl(method = "cv", number = 5, classProbs = TRUE, savePredictions = "final")

# Evaluation Metrics
eval_metrics <- function(truth, preds, model_name, palette) {
  acc <- mean(preds == truth)
  cat(paste0("\n", model_name, " - Accuracy: ", round(acc, 3), "\n\n"))

  # Confusion Matrix
  cm <- confusionMatrix(preds, truth)



    print(cm)

    # Precision, Recall, F1
    df <- tibble(truth = truth, prediction = preds)
    cat("\nPrecision:\n")
    print(precision(df, truth = truth, estimate = prediction))
    cat("\nRecall:\n")
    print(recall(df, truth = truth, estimate = prediction))
    cat("\nF1 Score:\n")
    print(f_meas(df, truth = truth, estimate = prediction))

    # Confusion Matrix Heatmap
    cm_table <- table(Truth = truth, Prediction = preds)
    cm_df <- as.data.frame(cm_table)

    ggplot(cm_df, aes(x = Prediction, y = Truth, fill = Freq)) +
      geom_tile(color = "white") +
      geom_text(aes(label = Freq), size = 5) +
      scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = palette) +
      labs(title = paste(model_name, "- Confusion Matrix Heatmap"),
           x = "Predicted Class", y = "Actual Class") +
      theme_minimal()
}

# Random Forest
set.seed(123)
rf_model <- caret::train(
  outcome ~ .,
  data = train_baked,
  method = "rf",
  trControl = ctrl,
  ntree = 200,
  importance = TRUE
)
rf_preds <- predict(rf_model, newdata = test_baked)
rf_probs <- predict(rf_model, newdata = test_baked, type = "prob")
eval_metrics(test_baked$outcome, rf_preds, "Random Forest", "deepskyblue")

# XGBoost
set.seed(123)
xgb_model <- train(
  outcome ~ .,
  data = train_baked,
  method = "xgbTree",
  trControl = ctrl,
  tuneLength = 3,
  verbose = TRUE
)
xgb_preds <- predict(xgb_model, newdata = test_baked)
xgb_probs <- predict(xgb_model, newdata = test_baked, type = "prob")
eval_metrics(test_baked$outcome, xgb_preds, "XGBoost", "mediumpurple")

# Neural Network
set.seed(123)
nnet_model <- train(
  outcome ~ .,
  data = train_baked,
  method = "nnet",
  trControl = ctrl,
  tuneLength = 5,
  trace = FALSE,
  maxit = 200
)
nnet_preds <- predict(nnet_model, newdata = test_baked)
nnet_probs <- predict(nnet_model, newdata = test_baked, type = "prob")
eval_metrics(test_baked$outcome, nnet_preds, "Neural Net", "hotpink")

# Stop parallel cluster
stopCluster(cl)
```

## RQ2: Clustering Hit Ball Locations

## Spectral Clustering

```{r spectral-clustering}
set.seed(123)

# Reduce dataset to 5000 samples for clustering
only_hits_reduced <- sample_n(only_hits, size = 5000)

# Visualize subsampled data
ggplot(data = only_hits_reduced,
       mapping = aes(hc_x, hc_y, colour = type_of_hit)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.2, size = 0.8) +
  scale_y_reverse() +
  coord_fixed() +
  labs(title = "Hit Coordinates of Batted Balls (Subset)", x = "X", y = "Y") +
  guides(colour = guide_legend(title = "Type of Hit", override.aes = list(alpha = 1))) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(panel.border = element_blank())

set.seed(123)

# Format for spectral clustering
loc_data <- only_hits_reduced %>% select(hc_x, hc_y)
loc_matrix <- as.matrix(loc_data)
loc_matrix <- scale(loc_matrix, scale = FALSE, center = TRUE)

# Perform spectral clustering

# Define k-nearest neighbors function
knn <- function(values, k) {
  index <- order(values, decreasing = TRUE)[(1:k) + 1]
  values[-index] <- NA
  return(values)
}

# Construct similarity and adjacency matrices
S <- exp(-1/2 * as.matrix(dist(loc_matrix)))
W <- apply(S, 2, function(x) knn(x, k = 20))
W <- (W + t(W)) / 2
W[is.na(W)] <- 0

# Compute Laplacian
D <- diag(colSums(W))
L <- D - W

# Eigen decomposition
n <- nrow(L)
ED <- eigen(L)

set.seed(123)

# Try different values of K for k-means
for(K in 1:6){
  # Find last K eigen vectors of L
  U = ED$vectors[, (n-K+1):n]
  # Run k-means on last K eigen vectors of L
  KM = kmeans(U, centers = K, iter.max = 100, nstart = 10)
  # Print output
  print(scatterplot(only_hits_reduced, KM$cluster))
}

set.seed(123)

# Plot clustered results
plt <- ggplot()+
  geom_point(data.frame(only_hits_reduced, cluster = as.factor(KM$cluster)),
             alpha=0.5, mapping = aes(hc_x, hc_y, color = cluster, shape=type_of_hit)) +
  scale_y_reverse() + labs(title = "Hit Coordinates of Batted Balls", x = "X", y = "Y") +
  guides(colour = guide_legend(override.aes = list(alpha = 1))) + theme_bw()
```

## RQ3: Key Factors Behind Player Performance

```{r rq3-key-factors-behind-player-performanc}
# Remove identifiers and irrelevant fields
data_q3 <- data_cleaned %>% select(-player_name, -batter, -pitcher, -des, -sv_id,
                                             -game_pk, -contains("fielder"), -game_year, -on_1b,
                                             -on_2b, -on_3b, -pitcher.1, -inning_topbot, -...1)

#  foul or strike (not the third). So, can impute “none” for missing variables here.
# Identify variable types
character_cols <- colnames(data_q3[, sapply(data_q3, class) == 'character'])
numeric_cols <- colnames(data_q3[, sapply(data_q3, class) == 'numeric'])

# Impute missing values
data_q3_imputed <- data_q3 %>%
  mutate_at(character_cols, ~replace_na(., "None")) %>%
  mutate_at(numeric_cols, ~replace_na(., 0))

# Select features fixed at time of bat
data_q3_reduced <- data_q3_imputed %>%
  select(pitch_type, release_speed, release_pos_x, release_pos_y, release_pos_z, zone,
         stand, p_throws, balls, strikes, pfx_x, pfx_z, plate_x, plate_z, outs_when_up,
         inning, vx0, vy0, vz0, ax, ay, az, sz_top, sz_bot, effective_speed,
         release_spin_rate, release_extension, launch_speed_angle, at_bat_number,
         pitch_number, pitch_name, home_score, away_score, bat_score, fld_score,
         if_fielding_alignment, of_fielding_alignment, spin_axis, team_at_bat, woba_value)

X <- data_q3_reduced %>% select(-woba_value)
y <- data_q3_reduced %>% select(woba_value)

# Find character columns
character_cols = colnames(X[, sapply(X, class) == 'character'])
numeric_cols = colnames(X[, sapply(X, class) == 'numeric'])

# Normalize numeric, one-hot encode categorical
X_numeric <- X[numeric_cols]
X_normalized <- scale(X_numeric)
y_normalized <- scale(y)

X_char <- X[character_cols]
X_char <- lapply(X_char, factor)
X_char <- as.data.table(X_char)
X_onehot <- one_hot(X_char)

X_full <- cbind(as.data.frame(X_numeric), as.data.frame(X_onehot))
# Calculate the correlation matrix
cor_matrix <- cor(X_numeric)
corrplot(cor_matrix, diag = FALSE, method = "color", type = "upper", tl.cex = 0.5,
         mar = c(1, 1, 2.5, 1))
```

## PCA

```{r pca}
# Run PCA and create scree plot
PCA <- prcomp(X_full, center = TRUE, scale. = FALSE)
summ <- summary(PCA)
ve <- summ$importance[2,]

barplot(ve) +
  labs(title = "Scree Plot of PCA") +
  xlab("Principal Component") +
  ylab("Variance Explained") +
  ylim(0, 1)
# Print cumulative variance
```

## Model Training: XGBoost

```{r model-training-xgboost}
set.seed(1013)
q3_pcs <- PCA$x[,1:2]
q3_pcs <- as.data.frame(cbind(q3_pcs, y_normalized))

# Data split
trainIndex <- createDataPartition(q3_pcs$woba_value, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)
train_data <- q3_pcs[trainIndex, ]
test_data <- q3_pcs[-trainIndex, ]

# Convert data to matrix format (XGBoost requires matrices)
train_matrix <- as.matrix(train_data[, 1:2])
test_matrix <- as.matrix(test_data[, 1:2])
train_label <- train_data$woba_value
test_label <- test_data$woba_value
# Convert data to XGBoost matrix format
dtrain <- xgb.DMatrix(data = as.matrix(train_data[, -which(names(train_data) == "woba_value")]),
                      label = train_data$woba_value)

# Define XGBoost parameters
params <- list(
  objective = "reg:squarederror",   # Regression task
  eta = 0.1,                        # Learning rate
  max_depth = 6,                    # Tree depth
  subsample = 0.5                   # Row sampling

)

# Perform 5-fold cross-validation
cv_results <- xgb.cv(
  params = params,
  data = dtrain,
  nrounds = 100,        # Number of boosting rounds
  nfold = 5,            # 5-fold CV
  metrics = "rmse",     # Evaluate using RMSE
  early_stopping_rounds = 10, # Stop early if no improvement
  verbose = FALSE
)

# Print best iteration based on CV results
best_nrounds <- cv_results$best_iteration

# Train final model with best nrounds
xgb_model <- xgboost(
  params = params,
  data = dtrain,
  nrounds = best_nrounds, # Use best number of rounds
  verbose = FALSE
)
test_pred <- predict(xgb_model, test_matrix)
test_data <- tibble(pred = test_pred, actual = test_label)

# Scatter plot of predictions vs actuals
ggplot(test_data, aes(x = pred, y = actual)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_abline()

# Compute RMSE
test_data %>%
  mutate(residuals = actual - pred) %>%
  summarize(rmse = sqrt(mean(residualsˆ2)))

# Find range of the outcomes to see if RMSE is decent

# Get feature importance from XGBoost model
importance_matrix <- xgb.importance(model = xgb_model)
print(importance_matrix)

# Plot feature importance
xgb.plot.importance(importance_matrix)

# Get PCA loadings
loadings <- PCA$rotation[, 1:2]   # Loadings for first 2 PCs

# Find top contributing original variables for the most important PCs
top_pcs <- importance_matrix$Feature[1:2] # Top 5 PCs from XGBoost
for (pc in top_pcs) {
  cat("\nTop contributing variables for", pc, ":\n")
  print(sort(abs(loadings[, pc]), decreasing = TRUE)[1:5]) # Show top 5 variables per PC
}

# Create a list to store the top contributing variables for each PC
top_pcs <- importance_matrix$Feature[1:2] # Top 2 PCs from XGBoost
bubble_data <- list()

for (pc in top_pcs) {
  # Get top 5 variables for the PC
  top_contrib <- sort(abs(loadings[, pc]), decreasing = TRUE)[1:5]
  top_contrib_df <- data.frame(
    Variable = names(top_contrib),
    Contribution = top_contrib,
    PC = rep(pc, length(top_contrib)),
    Size = abs(top_contrib) * 10
  )
  bubble_data[[pc]] <- top_contrib_df
}

# Combine all data frames into one
bubble_data_all <- do.call(rbind, bubble_data)
bubble_data_all <- bubble_data_all %>%
  mutate(vartext = case_when(
    Variable == "spin_axis" ~ "Pitch Spin Axis",
    Variable == "ax" ~ "Pitch Acceleration, X",
    Variable == "ay" ~ "Pitch Acceleration, Y",
    Variable == "az" ~ "Pitch Acceleration, Z",
    Variable == "vy0" ~ "Pitch Velocity, Y",
    Variable == "effective_speed" ~ "Pitch Speed from Extension",
    Variable == "release_spin_rate" ~ "Pitch Spin Rate"

           ))

# Create a bubble plot
ggplot(bubble_data_all, aes(x = reorder(vartext, Contribution), y = PC,
                            size = Size, color = PC)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(3, 10)) + #
  labs(title = "Bubble Plot of Top Contributing Variables for PCs",
       x = "Variable", y = "Principal Component", size = "Contribution", color = "PC") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

```
